title: witheringia mexicana
intro: Witheringia mexicana, also known as the Mexican jimsonweed, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. It is a climbing vine with medicinal properties that have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain solanine, which has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing pain, inflammation, and fever. Witheringia mexicana can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat colds, fever, and other respiratory issues, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
